description_long: This object consists of a group of nine small residential houses arranged in a loose grid. The houses feature simple rectangular forms with pitched gable roofs, and their exteriors are painted in muted tones of yellow, blue, gray, and white. Each house has a distinct front door and multiple rectangular windows, with some houses displaying symmetrical window arrangements. The roofs are textured to resemble shingles, and the doors and window frames are rendered in a contrasting dark color. The houses vary slightly in size and height, with some being single-story and others two-story. The overall appearance suggests a modest suburban or small-town neighborhood setting.
description: A group of nine small, gable-roofed houses in muted colors with doors and windows, arranged in a neighborhood cluster.
description_view_0: Front view showing the facades, doors, and window arrangements of all nine houses, highlighting their color and size variations.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the depth of the houses and the alignment of their roofs, with less visibility of doors and windows.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the backs and roofs of the houses, with minimal detail on doors or windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other ends of the houses, emphasizing their width and the arrangement in the cluster.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple single front door with no porch, steps, or decorative elements
